definition: All rows read from data/raw/segment_data.csv before deduplication.
how_to_read_it: This is the source inventory count.

definition: Rows after keeping the latest srcdate for each gvkey, datadate, stype, sid key.
how_to_read_it: This is the default denominator for the report series.

definition: Rows removed because a newer source snapshot exists for the same gvkey, datadate, stype, sid key.
how_to_read_it: This removes duplicate source snapshots; it is not a research-sample exclusion.

definition: Segment-level observations in the displayed group after latest-source deduplication.
how_to_read_it: Used to show raw table scale.

definition: Distinct gvkey values with at least one row in the displayed group.
how_to_read_it: Used to show the number of companies represented.

definition: Distinct gvkey and datadate combinations with at least one row in the displayed group.
how_to_read_it: Used to show panel support for regressions.

definition: Share of rows where a single field is not missing.
Share of rows with sales and ops present and sales not equal to zero.
how_to_read_it: Used for operating-margin and alpha-style analyses.

definition: Share of rows with sales, ops, capxs, and ias all present.
Rows with sales and emps present.
Rows with sales and rds present.
GEOSEG rows with sales and a nonblank segment name.
how_to_read_it: A screen for possible geography/currency exposure, not a complete causal design.

definition: Coverage within mechanical pre/event/post windows around candidate shocks.
how_to_read_it: A data support screen only; it does not prove identification.

definition: A gvkey, datadate, and stype combination.
how_to_read_it: Main unit for segment-count tables because business, geographic, and operating segment tables are overlapping reporting dimensions.

definition: All segment rows pooled across stype.
how_to_read_it: Included as an inventory check only. Paper tables should usually keep stype separate unless the design explicitly pools reporting dimensions.

definition: snms after trimming whitespace is not blank.
how_to_read_it: Used so blank names do not count as named segments.

definition: Rows whose segment name does not match corporate, elimination, unallocated, reconciliation, all-other, or similar nonoperating patterns.
how_to_read_it: A descriptive screen only. It is not a final sample rule.

definition: Positive segment sales divided by positive sales summed within gvkey, datadate, and stype.
how_to_read_it: Used to summarize concentration and segment size. Undefined when the firm-year-stype has no positive segment sales.
